### **Topic 2: Using Unit Rates at the Grocery Store**

**The Situation:** My mom sent me to the grocery store to buy a carton of orange juice. When I got there, I saw two different sizes from the same brand. To compare them fairly, I needed to find the **unit rate**, which in this case is the **price per ounce**.

    *   $5.49 ÷ 89 ounces ≈ $0.0616 per ounce (or about 6.2 cents per ounce)
